ddls:
CREATE TABLE "addresses" (
  "address_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each address */,
  "address" TEXT /* Primary address line */,
  "address2" TEXT /* Secondary address line */,
  "district" TEXT /* District or neighborhood */,
  "city_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the city where the address is located */,
  "postal_code" TEXT /* Postal code or zip code */,
  "phone" TEXT /* Phone number associated with the address */,
  "last_update" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update to the address */,
  "geolocation" TEXT /* Geographical coordinates (latitude and longitude) of the address */,
  "address_type" TEXT /* Type of the address (e.g., primary, secondary) */,
  "validation_status" TEXT /* Validation status of the address (e.g., valid, invalid, pending) */,
  "validation_date" TEXT /* Date when the address was last validated */,
  "source_system" TEXT /* Source system or source from which the address was imported */,
  "timezone" TEXT /* Timezone of the address */,
  "additional_notes" TEXT /* Additional notes or remarks about the address */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("address_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_addresses_city_id FOREIGN KEY ("city_id") REFERENCES cities ("city_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "cities" (
  "city_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each city */,
  "city_name" TEXT /* Name of the city */,
  "country_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the country where the city is located */,
  "last_update" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update to the city */,
  "timezone" TEXT /* Timezone of the city */,
  "population" INTEGER /* Population of the city */,
  "latitude" REAL /* Latitude of the city */,
  "longitude" REAL /* Longitude of the city */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("city_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_cities_country_id FOREIGN KEY ("country_id") REFERENCES countries ("country_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "countries" (
  "country_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each country */,
  "country_name" TEXT /* Name of the country */,
  "last_update" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update to the country */,
  "timezone" TEXT /* Primary timezone of the country */,
  "population" INTEGER /* Population of the country */,
  "latitude" REAL /* Latitude of the country */,
  "longitude" REAL /* Longitude of the country */,
  "currency" TEXT /* Primary currency used in the country */,
  "language" TEXT /* Primary language(s) spoken in the country */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("country_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "locations" (
  "location_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each location */,
  "location_type_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the type of location */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the location */,
  "address_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the address of the location */,
  "manager_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the manager of the location */,
  "last_update" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update to the location */,
  "status" TEXT /* Operational status of the location (e.g., active, closed) */,
  "opening_date" TEXT /* Date when the location was opened */,
  "closing_date" TEXT /* Date when the location was closed, if applicable */,
  "contact_email" TEXT /* Primary contact email for the location */,
  "contact_phone" TEXT /* Primary contact phone number for the location */,
  "timezone" TEXT /* Timezone of the location */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("location_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_locations_location_type_id FOREIGN KEY ("location_type_id") REFERENCES location_types ("location_type_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_locations_address_id FOREIGN KEY ("address_id") REFERENCES addresses ("address_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_locations_manager_id FOREIGN KEY ("manager_id") REFERENCES managers ("manager_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "location_types" (
  "location_type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each location type */,
  "type_name" TEXT /* Name of the location type */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the location type */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Indicates whether the location type is currently active or deprecated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("location_type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "managers" (
  "manager_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each manager */,
  "manager_name" TEXT /* Full name of the manager */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the manager */,
  "phone" TEXT /* Phone number of the manager */,
  "last_update" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update to the manager's information */,
  "timezone" TEXT /* Timezone of the manager */,
  "position" TEXT /* Position or title of the manager */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department to which the manager belongs */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("manager_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., admin, manager, staff) */,
  "last_update" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update to the user's information */,
  "timezone" TEXT /* Timezone of the user */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the user (e.g., active, suspended) */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department to which the user belongs */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Last login date and time of the user */,
  "failed_login_attempts" INTEGER /* Number of failed login attempts for the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "address_history" (
  "history_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each address change event */,
  "address_id" INTEGER /* ID of the address being changed */,
  "address" TEXT /* Primary address line */,
  "address2" TEXT /* Secondary address line */,
  "district" TEXT /* District or neighborhood */,
  "city_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the city where the address is located */,
  "postal_code" TEXT /* Postal code or zip code */,
  "phone" TEXT /* Phone number associated with the address */,
  "change_date" TEXT /* Date and time of the change */,
  "changed_by_user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who made the change */,
  "status_before" TEXT /* Status of the address before the change */,
  "status_after" TEXT /* Status of the address after the change */,
  "reason_for_change" TEXT /* Reason for the address change */,
  "approval_status" TEXT /* Approval status of the address change (e.g., pending, approved, rejected) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("history_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_address_history_changed_by_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("changed_by_user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_address_history_address_id FOREIGN KEY ("address_id") REFERENCES addresses ("address_id")
)
